content: {"personas": [{"perspective": "Scientific", "name": "Dr. Elena Rossi", "expertise": "Clinical endocrinologist and meta-analyst specializing in metabolic-syndrome trials. Author on GRADE-rated systematic reviews of time-restricted eating and alternate-day fasting.", "question_focus": "What pooled effect sizes on glycemic, lipid, anthropometric, and blood-pressure endpoints have the strongest GRADE-high certainty?"}, {"perspective": "Public_Health", "name": "Dr. Marcus Haverford", "expertise": "Cardiovascular epidemiologist with expertise in NHANES cohort analysis and observational study design. Methodologist in the debate around 2024 AHA EPI/Lifestyle time-restricted eating mortality abstract.", "question_focus": "How should clinicians reconcile short-term supervised RCT benefit with population-level cardiovascular mortality signals?"}, {"perspective": "Regional", "name": "Dr. Fatima Al-Rashid", "expertise": "Clinical nutritionist at a Gulf-region academic medical center specializing in Ramadan-fasting physiology in Muslim adults with diabetes and metabolic syndrome.", "question_focus": "What does the Ramadan IF systematic-review evidence contribute as a real-world counterweight to Western supervised trials?"}, {"perspective": "Industry", "name": "Rachel Kim, PharmD, MPH", "expertise": "Health-economics and regulatory affairs specialist focused on commercial IF apps, FTC substantiation, and consumer-vs-clinical claim distinctions.", "question_focus": "How do real-world adherence and retention data from commercial IF apps compare with supervised-RCT effect sizes?"}, {"perspective": "Emerging_Trends", "name": "Dr. Anders Lindqvist", "expertise": "Translational researcher in chrononutrition, gut microbiome-immune signaling, and adipose tissue remodeling. Active in IL-22/ILC3 beigeing literature.", "question_focus": "Which mechanistic pathways most plausibly explain clinical benefits and where does the animal-to-human translation gap sit?"}]}
